title: Embedded Systems I for Science Students
outline: This course gives Science students majoring in Computer Engineering a strong foundation in embedded systems, introducing digital system fundamentals including: information representation, Boolean algebra, logic gate behaviour, combinational and sequential digital circuits, digital building blocks and algorithmic state machines; C programming with a focus on microcontroller applications; and basic microcontroller usage including an introduction to computer architecture, general purpose input/outputs, analogue to digital convertors and basic timers.
